title: Finding the Area of a Triangle

steps.instruction: The area of a triangle is A = 1/2 × base × height.
steps.explanation: The base and height must be perpendicular to each other.

steps.instruction: A triangle has base 8 cm and height 5 cm.
steps.explanation: Substitute the given measurements into the formula.
First multiply base × height, then take half.

title: Finding a Missing Dimension from Area

steps.instruction: A triangle has area 30 sq cm and base 12 cm. Find the height.
steps.explanation: We know the area and base, so we solve for height.
This simplifies the equation to one step.
